summary: Updated the repository Makefile linker tool version setting, changing `MWCC_LD_VERSION` from `1.1` to `1.3.2`. The PR was a minimal build-tooling maintenance change with no comments or review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: In `Makefile`, the linker version variable was updated from `MWCC_LD_VERSION := 1.1` to `MWCC_LD_VERSION := 1.3.2`.
The existing compiler-related version variables `MWCC_VERSION := 1.2.5` and `MWCC_EPI_VERSION := 1.2.5n` were left unchanged.
smart_moves: Kept the change narrowly scoped to the single build variable that controls the `mwld` version, reducing risk of unrelated build-system churn.
Used the existing Makefile convention for tool version declarations rather than introducing a new variable or mechanism.
decomp_lessons: Toolchain version pins are part of matching infrastructure; even a one-line Makefile update can matter for reproducible decompilation builds.
When updating CodeWarrior-related tooling, preserve the existing variable naming pattern and keep compiler, EPI compiler, and linker version pins distinct.
naming_conventions: `MWCC_VERSION` is used for the main CodeWarrior compiler version pin.
`MWCC_EPI_VERSION` is used for the EPI compiler version pin.
`MWCC_LD_VERSION` is used for the linker, titled in the PR as `mwld`, version pin.
Version variables in the Makefile use uppercase snake case with the `MWCC_` prefix.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs or issues mentioning `MWCC_LD_VERSION` or `mwld 1.3.2` to understand why the linker bump was needed.
Check whether the linker version bump affected matching, map generation, or platform-specific build behavior.
